title: Introduction
description: Introduction to the book Yoga Sutra of Master Patanjali.

title: The First Corner Stone
description: Introduction to the first chapter of the book - The Chapter on Meditation.

title: The Power of Compassion
description: Short review of how we can become whole in term of being.

title: To Become Whole
description: Practice of the mind to become whole in term of being.

title: The Seer
description: The experience of first time seeing the ultimate reality when we are in deep metation.

title: A Day in The Mind
description: The goal of Yoga is not to stop all thoughts. We simply want to stop the Great Mistake, and all the unhappiness it causes.

title: Pictures in The Mind
description: Imaginations and Recollections are just mental pictures in the mind without corresponded concrete outer object.

title: Approaching The Door
description: Stopping the Great Mistake requires constant practice, giving up attachments, and striving to be there - the first nirvana.

title: The Power of Daily Practice
description: Our spiritual practice must become a regular part of our day. The mind is infinitely powerful. We can learn to be good at anything, if only we give it an hour of two practices a day. But every day.

title: Attachment to Distraction
description: Giving up attachment to distraction consists of decision to gain control over craving for experiences, seen, or only heard of.

title: Attachment to Illusion
description: The worse attachment of all is to be attached to the idea that the things all around us exist out there on their own, concretely, in the sense that they don't depend on how we lead our life.

title: Meditation Traps
description: Praticing meditation without being infected by the Great Mistake can save our world. We need guidance from holy Masters and their blessings.

title: Karmic Seeds That Never Sprout
description: The factor that makes negative seeds sprout is simply seeing things the wrong way. When we practice meditation well and avoid meditation traps, we can prevent karmic seeds from sprouting.

title: The Five Powers
description: The powers we must first use: Awareness, Meditation, Wisdom, Belief, and Effort.

title: The Four Stages
description: On the Path of Preparation, we will pass through four stages which prepare us for te next path. These four stages are: Warmth, Peek, Mastery, and the Highest Object of All.

title: The Transcended Beings
description: And another way is to ask Transcended Beings for their blessing. A Trancended Being is an ordinary-extra person who is untouched by mental afflictions and by deeds.

title: Serve The Masters
description: It's a kind of magic that happens when we've found back our own Masters and commit ourselves to the joy of working closely with a spiritual guidance, serve the Masters and their sacred work.

title: The Highest of Prayers
description: Calling upon their names is the first of all prayers. We must repeat this prayers and think well upon the meaning. With this, we will gain the ability to focus the mind within, and to avoid all obstacles.

title: Beginning Obstacles
description: Unpleasantness of the body is obviously an obstacle but it's also a spiritual practice. It inspires us to work on what's really important in life, and makes us more humble and sympathic.

title: Ultimate Obstacles
description: The ultimate obstacles come from mistaken views of the existence which are left incorrected, failing to reach specific levels of practice, or not being established firmly.

title: Inner and Outer
description: Yoga is also the union of the inner and outer methods for reaching total purity. This union depends upon the connection between our physical outer body and our spiritual inner presence.

title: The Four Infinite Thoughts
description: The crucial practice which can help us pass through all obstacles is called the Four Infinite Thoughts. They're called Infinite because - in the end - we'll see infinite creatures on infinite worlds with our own mind in a sense of infinite compassion for a moment.

title: Bright and Clear
description: This practice makes the mind bright and clear like crystal. It gives the same effect as releasing the breath. A daily meditation on the Four Infinite Thoughts changes our entire life. It gives our life real and lasting meaning.

title: Freedom from Ego
description: It also makes our mind free from desires and our presence radiant like starlight. We are freed forever from selfishness and from wanting anything other than the Ultimate Compassion.

title: The Deeper Powers
description: It more over enables us to stay conscious in our dream when we have to sleep. It brings us to the same exhilaration as deep meditation does. We may gain mastery over tinies atoms and hugest galaxies as well.

title: The Path of Diamond
description: Those extra-ordinary people who shatter the way the mind turn things around, use a balanced meditation, which is fixed and clear on its object. And the object is like a crystal, with the one that holds it, and what it holds, and the holding itself as well.

title: Remember What You See
description: When we grasp an object by mixing up the word into the image, then that is the type of balanced meditation. Staying in that one pure thought and never forget it: Things are empty of what they are by themselves. This is the clear light beyond all conceptual thoughts.

title: Approaching The Goal
description: The day after we see the Ultimate Reality, we are on the fourth path - the Path of Habituation. It's called this because we are getting used to what we saw - using that indescriable experience to complete the work of dissolving negative karmic seeds in our mind forever.

title: Beyond All Fear
description: When we gain the fearlessness of going beyond all examining, we reach inner bliss. At this point, wisdom becomes vast and awakened. We experience a completely different object than with the wisdoms of hearing and reasoning, because what we see is far beyond.

title: The End of Karmic Seeds
description: The mental karmic seeds thus created cuts off all other seeds. And when mental seeds are surpressed this way, everything is stopped. Thus it is known as deep meditation where we no longer have those karmic seeds.

title: The Second Cornerstone
description: In the first chapter, we use deep meditation to travel through the five paths. In the second chapter, we start some very practical yoga methods to attain this meditation and the wisdom which rides it.

title: The True Enemy
description: The five negative thoughts are: Ignorance, Selfness, Grasping, and ignorantly Liking or Disliking. Ignorance is the field for the ones after it, whether they are dormant, dwindling, interrupted, or flourishing.

title: The Four Mistake
description: In ignorance, we misunderstand about things in our world: things that cannot last, things which are impure, things which are painful, and things which are not there by themselves.

title: Beginning of Self
description: We enter life with the seed of ignorance within us. And then in the very womb, the karmic seeds flowers into a personal experience of this mis-understanding, which is here called Selfness. This wrong idea of Me is awakened by our very first sensations in life. Inflicted by ignorance, the mind immediately splits life into Feeling and Me Feeling.

title: Is It Wrong to Like Things ?
keywords: is it wrong to like things
description: It's not wrong to like things. Spiritually advanced people enjoy things so much more than we can. And the very ability to like and dislike gets us to the path of seeking enlightenment.

title: Healing Our World
description: If we want to stop suffering and pains, we have to surpress the negative karmic seeds. And this is done in the mind, in deep meditation, beginning with the seeds that causes the Great Mistake.

title: Where Things Come From
description: Negative thoughts are the very root of the storehouse, planted by the things we do. And then we experience things in lifetime we see or not. As long as this root is still there, then we will experience the ripening of these actions in our lives to come.

title: Where Sufferings and Pains Come from ?
keywords: where sufferings and pains come from
description: There is a connection of causes and effects. The karmic seeds ripen into experiences refreshingly pleasant or painful in their torment.

title: Why Things Fall Apart ?
description: Everything comes from the karmic seeds, and seeds die by sprouting. Truly then, every part of our lives - even the good things - must one day cause us suffering and pains. This is the second truth - the Truth of Suffering and Pains.

title: Why We Suffer ?
description: Karmic seeds are planted in the mind - simply by being aware that we are doing, or saying, or thinking something towards someone else. The seeds enter the storehouse and wait to be called up, like airplane standing in the line to take off. Certain seeds, like priority flights, get to move ahead of the others in line.

title: Everything We See
description: What we see, what appears to us, is the state of all things, something to take in or to use for our liberation.

title: The Two Reality
description: For convenience of working with the mind, we can see the universe as two parts - two realities: Deceptive and Ultimate

title: The Loneliness of Seeing
description: This thing belong to a person who has seen. What is dissolved for one who have reached this goal, is not dissolved for others - for they still possess the foundation.

title: To See The Illusion
description: People possessed of discrimination which come from revelation are no longer at a loss. They now have a method to accomplish this dissolution and carry them to the end of seventh level.

title: The Eight Limb
description: The Eight Limbs are: Self-aware, Commitments, Yoga-poses, Breath-control, Senses-guard, Focus, Fixation, and Perfect-meditation.

title: Dissolving Negative Karmic Seeds
description: When the image starts to hurt, sit down and work the antidote. The image - people who hurt us or the like - come from what we did ourselves - or got others to do for us - or what we was glad to hear that others had done.

title: The Four Forces
description: The antidote is called the Four Forces: Sit down first, and quietly review all our understandings about karmic seeds. Secondly, give some intelligent regret - not guilt - about how this action and its karmic seed will delay our perfect destiny. Thirdly, decide not to repeat the mistake. The fourth force is to take a positive action to counteract the negative one, mentally, physically.

title: In Our Presence
description: If we make it a way of life never to hurt others, then in our presence, all conflicts come to an end.

title: Relationship and Possessiveness
description: For keeping sexual purity, a simple rule of thumb is always to act around another person's partner as if that another person were standing there too. It's like respecting other people's things.

title: Simply Clean
description: If we stay clean, then we can see our true-self. All the qualities needed are - Truth, Purity, Compassionate thoughts, Single-pointedness, and Mastery of senses.

title: How to Be Happy ?
description: If we stay content, then we archieve true happiness - which is unsurpassed. The karmic seeds for being happy and for accumulating wealth are different. The seeds that are planted by training ourselves to be satisfied with any level of material comfort is the ones for being happy.

title: Find Our Guardian
description: If we engage in regular study, then we come to be with the Transcended Beings of our deepest dreams. If we seek our Masters' blessing, we will attain final meditation.

title: Body Yoga
description: The yoga poses bring a feeling of well-being which stays with us. They do so through a balance of effort and relaxation - and through endless forms of meditation.

title: The Lie of Choices
description: And there will come a time when difference no longer harass you.

title: The End of Breath
description: The breath is mastered when - as we remain - the passing of breath in and out simply stops.

title: How to Breath
description: Keep a close watch on the breath - outside or inside, paused or being exchanged. Observe too the place in the body - the duration and the count, long and fine.

title: Breathing to A Single Point
description: The fourth step is when one has given up the outside, inside, and the experience itself. And then one can dissolve the veil that covers the light. The mind is fit for focus.

title: Ending The Tyranny of Stimulation
description: Learn to withdraw the mind from physical senses. Freed from its ties to outer objects, the mind can arrive at its own real nature. And with that, we attain the highest control of senses.

title: The Human Linga
description: The human body that we're possessing is a living linga. The word Linga literally means a temple or a device to perform spiritual practices, to communicate with others on higher realm, to learn and advance itself into higher capability being.

title: The Third Cornerstone
description: Locking the mind on an object is focus. And staying on that object over a stretch of time is fixation.

title: The Clear Light
description: Perfect meditation, then see this same object as simple as itself - it's clear light and totally void of any true nature of its own.
